# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 159

This is turn 159. Turns 1 through 158 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 159.

## Turn 159 — serialized recurrence DAG and lowest-failure descent become executable

The frozen \(h_0\) submission is unchanged: 256 bytes, SHA-256  
`4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc`. The tame relation remains implicit, and `40491355905` is the verifier residue of the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega_2\). 

I implemented Turn 158’s requested generic serialized-node front end and recurrence-arithmetic layer. The validator loads a file-per-node certificate DAG and checks, recursively and child-first:

- SHA-bound Proposition-I, II, and III reports;
- SHA-bound target-incidence reports;
- complete framed-node identities rather than abstract group isomorphism classes;
- actual embedded subgroup and Fourier-label multiplicities;
- strict marked-kernel descent;
- all four frozen recurrence branches and their divisibility, integrality, sign, and range constraints.

The arithmetic layer implements:

\[
\begin{array}{c|l}
\text{branch}&\text{validated recurrence}\\ \hline
R=1&E(Y)=|M|^2E(C)-P\\
|R|\ge4&
m_\lambda=S_\lambda/8,\quad
N_0=\dfrac{2\sum_{\lambda\ne0}m_\lambda+(2-d)E(B)}d,\quad
E(Y)=|R|^2dN_0\\
R=C_2,\ \mathrm{edge}\ne0&E(Y)=4E(B),\quad E(B)\ \text{even}\\
R=C_2,\ \mathrm{edge}=0&E(Y)=8Z-P
\end{array}
\]

For the last branch it independently checks the \(Z\)-formula, every \(8\mid S_a\), the flattened recurrence identity, and

\[
0\le Z\le |M|^2E(C),\qquad 0\le P\le 8Z.
\]

This is the nonnegative arithmetic shell audited in Turns 143–144, now attached to executable serialized nodes rather than retained as manuscript algebra. 

### End-to-end fixture

A nine-node certificate exercises the terminal case and every active branch. Its root evaluates to

\[
E(\mathrm{root})=928.
\]

The fixture genuinely exercises the delicate features:

- the \(|R|\ge4\) Fourier branch uses \(d=4\), so the negative coefficient \(2-d=-2\) is active;
- both determinant signs \(\varepsilon=+1\) and \(\varepsilon=-1\) occur;
- Fourier and scalar pushout sums are retained by label;
- embedded subgroup incidences retain multiplicity.

Eight independent corruptions were then introduced. All eight were rejected at their expected lowest node: Proposition I, Proposition II, Proposition III, target incidence, Fourier divisibility, zero-fibre arithmetic, duplicate incidence identity, and a wrong claimed root count. The validator therefore distinguishes source-interface failures, target-structure failures, and arithmetic failures rather than merely rejecting the root.

The fixtures are branch-coverage models, not additional casewise evidence for the presentation. The uniform gain is an executable localization pipeline for any supplied verifier-target certificate.

Artifacts:

- [Complete Turn 159 reproducibility bundle](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn159_recurrence_bundle.zip)
- [Generic recurrence validator](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn159_recurrence_bundle/turn159_recurrence_validator.py)
- [Machine-readable aggregate report](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn159_recurrence_bundle/turn159_report.json)
- [Exact frozen \(h_0\) submission](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn159_recurrence_bundle/q2_galois_h0_submission.txt)

Bundle SHA-256:

`c589e5db70380bb272e01e4bfb12d79bc4bb866e832a2bfc8d8160b7e8280d49`

**ESTABLISHED**: Implemented and reran a generic serialized certificate-DAG front end with the complete nonnegative recurrence arithmetic. It covers the terminal node and all four active branches, preserves embedded-incidence and Fourier-label multiplicities, enforces strict descent, and localizes eight deliberate defects to their lowest rejected source, incidence, or arithmetic node. The frozen \(h_0\) word and checksum are unchanged.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The new engine validates and evaluates supplied certificates; it does not yet synthesize the concrete root certificate, active block, Fourier covers, and subgroup-incidence data directly from an arbitrary target multiplication table.

**NEXT**: Build the uniform multiplication-table-to-certificate compiler. Given a first failing verifier target, it should construct the framed root, discover or reject an admissible active block, enumerate labeled Fourier covers and embedded exact-image children, serialize the resulting DAG, and feed it directly into this validator.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
